title: What did I think about distribution and going to market across all my notes?
html:
Question

"What did I think about distribution and going to market across all my notes?"

Synthesized answer

Yahir's distribution thinking has compounded across roughly six years, and the throughline is unmistakable: he keeps trying to collapse the surface area of his offer until it can be sold without explaining itself.

The earliest fingerprint is in 2019 08 12 keep things simple, where he writes "Have an elevator pitch for your self" and frames pitching as a "two way road — investors and invented. Dating." GTM, at this stage, is a personal-charisma problem: know your first minute cold. There's no channel theory yet, just the intuition that distribution begins at the moment of first contact.

By 2022 (2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream), the thinking gets more strategic. Buried in the same junk-drawer where he flags ChatGPT as a wedge are nonobvious ("Seek nonobvious things"), asking the right question ("the next best skill to have"), and a Portland agency hit-list (PixelSpoke, Vacasa, Ride Report, et al.) that reads like an early outreach target set. Distribution is starting to look like a list of named accounts, not a vibe.

In 2024 (2024 05 13 things), the thinking gets sharper still. The "decide on" startup-thesis menu repeats three times across the note — asset tokenization, smart-home integration, "Disrupting security and wasteful human interfaces" — and is paired with the star-rated "mindless boring tasks irl" inventory (parking attendant ★★★★, toll booth ★★★★★, gym front desk ★★). This is Yahir cataloging exact human jobs he wants to dissolve into software. Distribution is being thought about as which incumbent workflow do I replace?, not who do I email?

The BroMenu spec pivots back to direct-to-consumer instincts — TikTok marketing, Brotein "werun" group, leaderboards, group share — but at the same time embeds no runway discipline into technical decisions ("avoid the Apple Developer fee," "cache responses wherever possible"). The DTC posture is real but constrained.

Then in early 2026 (Rankett GTM Bible + 2026 01 13 rankett com), the thinking finally locks. The avatar narrows from "agencies and direct clients" to "agencies with 5+ existing clients." The offer collapses to one number: $998/seat/month. The architecture commits to ghost white label (zero Rankett branding anywhere), the moat becomes pooled data network effect, and the filter becomes the 60 day activation rule. Distribution is no longer a question of channels — it's the shape of the product. Sell to the people who already have the relationships; let them sell to everyone else; ride their compounding case studies into the next deal.

The arc, in one sentence: from "have an elevator pitch" → to "pick the sharpest possible avatar and let your customers be your distribution."

Sources

  • 2019 08 12 keep things simple — earliest pitching/distribution instincts
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — nonobvious filter, PDX agency hit-list, early ChatGPT wedges
  • 2024 05 13 things — "decide on" thesis menu, mindless human interface disruption inventory
  • 2024 12 21 todos — BroMenu DTC spec, TikTok/leaderboard distribution layer
  • 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide — the most articulated GTM doc in the corpus
  • 2026 01 13 rankett com — "We whitelabel ai search optimization"
  • whitelabel strategy · ghost white label · pooled data network effect · 60 day activation rule · ai visibility engine
  • elevator pitch · disruption · asking the right question · nonobvious · no runway discipline
